slides.notes:
Physics 331 opening — auto-reveal only, no clicks.

On-enter sequence (all trigger automatically):
  1. Grid frame + course/lecture tags
  2. Title 'Quantum Mechanics' slides up (drafting gold on ink blue — blueprint aesthetic, not synthwave)
  3. Sky-blue subtitle lines appear
  4. Pitch paragraph
  5. Ĥψ = Eψ scales into the drafting-gold box

Take a beat (≈2s) after step 5 before advancing to s2. Palette intent: engineer's blueprint / drafting desk.

elements.alt: Abstract quantum waveform — photonic wave patterns evoking wave-particle duality

elements.content: An intuition for wave–particle duality, the Schrödinger equation,
and why the uncertainty principle isn't about your microscope.
Physics at the atomic scale is strange. But the strangeness has structure — six slides, three equations, one revolution in how we model reality.

slides.notes:
Double-slit experiment. On enter: section tag, title, electron source dot (no click yet).

[step:1]Two rays leave the source, heading toward the barrier.[/step]

[step:2]3-segment barrier appears with "slit 1" / "slit 2" labels.[/step]

[step:3]Propagation rays spread from both slits toward the screen — the which-path ambiguity moment.[/step]

[step:4]Detector screen on the right.[/step]

[step:5]Interference pattern — 9 opacity-varied stripes fade in, bright / dim / bright / dim.[/step]

[step:6]Right-side commentary panel opens — "setup, paradox". Read it while the audience sees the stripes.[/step]

[step:7]Bottom Bohr quote: "Anyone who is not shocked by quantum theory has not understood it."[/step]

Key line: the screen records dots one at a time, yet the pattern is wave-like. The wave tells you where it might land; the particle is what arrives.

elements.content:
Fire electrons at two slits. On the screen beyond, they land in an *interference pattern* — the signature of two overlapping waves.

Yet the screen records point-like arrivals, one electron at a time.

The wave tells you *where* it might land. The particle is what arrives.

slides.notes:
Schrödinger equation. On enter: title, equation iℏ∂/∂t Ψ = ĤΨ fades into the gold-bordered box (auto).

[step:1]Symbols table slides in on the left. Walk each row: Ψ (wave function, complex), |Ψ|² (probability density — the ONLY observable), Ĥ (Hamiltonian = KE + V), ℏ (reduced Planck constant ≈ 10⁻³⁴ J·s).[/step]

[step:2]Right-side "Stationary Solutions" panel — when Ĥ is time-independent, the equation separates into Ĥψₙ = Eₙψₙ → discrete energies → atoms, bonds, spectral lines.[/step]

Punchline: the equation isn't about forces like F=ma. It's about amplitude waves.

elements.content:
When $\hat{H}$ is time-independent, the equation separates:

$$ \hat{H}\psi_n = E_n \psi_n $$

The allowed energies $E_n$ are *eigenvalues*; the $\psi_n$ are orthonormal eigenstates. This is where discreteness comes from — atoms, bonds, spectral lines.

slides.notes:
Heisenberg's uncertainty principle. On enter: title, Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ/2 scales in (left), TikZ wave packet on the right (sky-blue envelope + gold oscillation + Δx bracket).

[step:1]Bottom-left panel "NOT A MEASUREMENT LIMIT" — position and momentum are Fourier conjugates, narrowing one necessarily widens the other.[/step]

[step:2]Bottom-right panel "PRACTICAL CONSEQUENCES" — zero-point energy, tunneling, why electrons can't sit at the nucleus.[/step]

Timing: linger on the wave-packet graphic when explaining step 1. The visual IS the argument.

elements.content: Position and momentum aren't hidden properties we fail to measure. They're Fourier conjugates of the same wave — narrowing one *necessarily* widens the other.
Zero-point energy, tunneling, the stability of atoms (electrons can't sit still at the nucleus) — all follow from this single inequality.

slides.notes:
Particle in a box. On enter: title, two walls slide in at x=0 and x=L, floor line + axis label auto-reveal.

[step:1]n=1 energy line + half-sine wave function — lowest energy, one antinode.[/step]

[step:2]n=2 level + full sine (one node in the middle).[/step]

[step:3]n=3 level + three half-waves (two nodes).[/step]

[step:4]n=4 level + four half-waves. Spacing grows as n².[/step]

[step:5]Right panel header "Energy quantisation" + formula Eₙ = n²π²ℏ² / (2mL²) scales in.[/step]

[step:6]Right panel body — shrink L → energies spread apart, heavier m → energies compress to classical continuum.[/step]

Key insight: confinement forces discrete wavelengths, which forces discrete energies. No magic, just boundary conditions.

elements.content: # Particle in a box — the simplest solvable case

Confining a particle to a region of size $L$ forces its wave to have wavelengths that fit integer half-cycles — and forces its energy to be discrete.

Shrink $L$ → energies spread apart.
Make $m$ larger → energies compress toward classical continuum.

slides.notes:
Closing — quantum is the OS of reality. On enter: chalkboard background overlay, section tag, title, subtitle.

[step:1]Card 1: Transistors. Band gaps, p-n junctions, tunneling current. A 5-nm MOSFET is a quantum device.[/step]

[step:2]Card 2: Lasers. Stimulated emission + population inversion → coherent light from discrete energy levels.[/step]

[step:3]Card 3: MRI. Nuclear spin precession in a magnetic field. Maps hydrogen density non-invasively.[/step]

[step:4]Card 4: Your DNA. Hydrogen bonds, base pairing. Chemistry is applied quantum mechanics.[/step]

[step:5]Horizontal rule + Bohr quote appears — "Anyone who is not shocked by quantum theory has not understood it."[/step]

Landing tone: don't oversell. The equations look intimidating but they describe reality with vanishingly small error.

elements.alt: Moody old university physics chalkboard with faint erased equations — atmospheric classroom texture

elements.content: # Quantum is the OS of reality
Every piece of modern technology is a quantum effect, engineered into existence.
Band gaps, p-n junctions, and tunneling current — a 5-nm MOSFET is a quantum device.
Stimulated emission and population inversion — discrete energy levels turned into coherent light.
Nuclear spin precession in a magnetic field. Maps hydrogen density in soft tissue non-invasively.
Hydrogen bonds and base pairing — chemistry is just applied quantum mechanics.
*"Anyone who is not shocked by quantum theory has not understood it."*  — Niels Bohr
